migration_policy: additive until 1.x; breaking changes require migration note, fixture update, backwards-compatibility decision, explicit reviewer approval
description: Registry of specific, catalogued exam misconceptions that CC distractors target. Each entry maps a concrete wrong belief to (a) a category from the controlled misconception taxonomy in bank-item.schema.json, and (b) the objective where it bites. Seeded PR-8a from the PR-7 adversarial review (reports/pr7-adversarial-review.md §6): a confirmed accuracy defect in a fact card is, by definition, a misconception a real candidate can hold. Gold-item distractors and wrong-answer rationales reference these so a wrong answer teaches the correction, not just 'incorrect'.

entries.misconception: The incident-response lifecycle has six phases.
entries.correct: Four phases; Containment, Eradication & Recovery is one combined phase.

entries.misconception: A threat is always external; insiders are not threats.
entries.correct: Threats may be internal or external; insider threat is a tested concept.

entries.misconception: Hashing is a form of encryption and can be reversed.
entries.correct: Hashing is one-way; it provides integrity, not confidentiality, and cannot be reversed.

entries.misconception: RTO can be longer than MTD.
entries.correct: RTO must be equal to or less than MTD.

entries.misconception: In MAC the owner grants access; in DAC the system enforces labels.
entries.correct: DAC is owner-controlled; MAC is system-enforced by classification labels.

entries.misconception: UDP guarantees ordered, reliable delivery like TCP.
entries.correct: TCP is connection-oriented and reliable; UDP is connectionless and best-effort.
